seoTitle: Create a Mobile Layout View for Phone-Ready Power BI Reports
metaDescription: Learn how to build a mobile layout view in Power BI so your reports look great and stay usable on phones and other small screens.
intro: A report that looks great on desktop can become unreadable on a phone without a dedicated mobile layout. This guide covers setting up a mobile-friendly canvas in Power BI, customizing visuals for smaller screens, and making your reports genuinely phone-ready.

steps.heading: Get an overview of mobile layouts
steps.body: Understand how Power BI's mobile layout view works as a separate canvas designed specifically for smaller screens.

steps.heading: Create a mobile layout view
steps.body: Switch into mobile layout mode and begin arranging the visuals you want visible on phone-sized screens.

steps.heading: Customize visuals for smaller screens
steps.body: Resize and reposition your visuals within the mobile layout so they remain readable and functional without excessive scrolling or zooming.

steps.heading: Explore use cases for mobile reports
steps.body: Mobile layouts are especially valuable for decision-makers who need quick access to insights while away from their desks, such as in sales or field service roles.

steps.heading: Optimize for faster accessibility
steps.body: Keep the mobile view focused on your most essential visuals so it loads quickly and stays useful for on-the-go review.

faq.question: Do I need to rebuild my whole report to create a mobile layout?
faq.answer: No, the mobile layout is a separate view built from your existing report visuals, so you're rearranging rather than recreating them.

faq.question: Can I choose which visuals appear in the mobile layout?
faq.answer: Yes, you can select which visuals to include, letting you prioritize the most important information for smaller screens.

faq.question: Will the mobile layout look the same on every phone?
faq.answer: The layout is designed to adapt reasonably well across devices, though screen size differences can still affect the exact viewing experience.

faq.question: Is a mobile layout necessary if my report already looks okay on phones?
faq.answer: A dedicated mobile layout still helps by letting you intentionally prioritize and arrange visuals rather than relying on automatic resizing.
